Here is the complete and thorough analysis of the Next.js project at `/home/user/workspace`.

---

## 1. Full Directory Tree (Top 5 Levels)

```
/home/user/workspace/
├── .gitignore                                    (412 B)

├── package-lock.json                             (151 KB)
├── pages/
│   ├── 404.js                                    (588 B)
│   ├── _app.js                                   (602 B)
│   ├── _document.js                              (584 B)
│   ├── _error.js                                 (714 B)
│   ├── index.js                                  (1.3 KB)
│   ├── api/
│   │   └── posts/
│   │       ├── [id].js                           (984 B)
│   │       └── index.js                          (837 B)
│   └── blog/
│       ├── [id].js                               (1.9 KB)
│       └── index.js                              (1.1 KB)
├── styles/
│   └── globals.css                               (1.4 KB)
└── tsconfig.json                                 (667 B)
```

The project has no `public/` directory, no `layouts/` directory, no `lib/`, no `middleware.ts`, and no `next-env.d.ts` (it is gitignored).

---

## 2. package.json -- Dependencies

```json
{
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next start"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@ai-sdk/react": "2.0.0-beta.28",
    "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.17.0",
    "ai": "5.0.59",
    "next": "16.2.0-canary.41",
    "react": "19.1.0",
    "react-dom": "19.1.0",
    "zod": "^4.0.10"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^20",
    "@types/react": "^19",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19",
    "typescript": "^5",
    "vitest": "^3.1.3",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.4.1",
    "vite-tsconfig-paths": "^5.1.4"
  }
}
```

**Key takeaways:**
- Next.js version: **16.2.0-canary.41** (a canary build, not a stable release)
- React version: **19.1.0**
- Uses ESM (`"type": "module"`)
- Notable extra dependencies: `ai` SDK v5, `@ai-sdk/react`, `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, `zod` v4
- Testing: `vitest` v3 with `@vitejs/plugin-react`

---

## 3. tsconfig.json

File: `/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json` (667 B)

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2017",
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "incremental": true,
    "plugins": [{"name": "next"}],
    "paths": {"@/*": ["./*"]}
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}
```

Notable: `"allowJs": true` is set because most source files are plain `.js`, not `.ts`/`.tsx`. The `@/*` path alias maps to the project root (`./*`).

---

## 4. All Files in `pages/`

### pages/_app.js (602 B)
The custom App component. Wraps all pages with:
- `AppProvider` (from `../components/AppProvider`) -- a React Context providing theme state
- A layout shell: `<header>` with site title "My Blog" and nav links to Home and Blog, a `<main>` wrapper, and a `<footer>` with copyright
- Global CSS import

### pages/_document.js (584 B)
Custom Document. Sets:
- `<Html lang="en">`
- Meta description, favicon link, Google Fonts preconnect and Inter font loading
- Standard `<Main />` / `<NextScript />` body structure

### pages/_error.js (714 B)
Custom error page using `getInitialProps`. Handles both server-side errors (from `res.statusCode`) and client-side errors (from `err.statusCode`). Renders a 404-specific message or a generic "something went wrong" message.

### pages/404.js (588 B)
Standalone custom 404 page. Uses `useRouter` to provide a "Go Back Home" button.

### pages/index.js (1.3 KB)
Home page. Uses **`getServerSideProps`** (SSR) to:
- Read the `user-agent` header from the request
- Fetch 5 posts from jsonplaceholder.typicode.com
- Return posts, userAgent, and server timestamp as props

Renders welcome message, timestamp, user-agent, and a list of recent post links. Has a "View All Posts" button using `router.push('/blog')`.

### pages/blog/index.js (1.1 KB)
Blog listing page. Uses **`getStaticProps`** with **ISR** (`revalidate: 60`) to:
- Fetch all posts from jsonplaceholder
- Revalidate every 60 seconds

Renders a grid of post cards with titles, truncated bodies, and "Read More" buttons.

### pages/blog/[id].js (1.9 KB)
Individual blog post page. Uses **`getStaticPaths`** with `fallback: 'blocking'` and **`getStaticProps`** with ISR (`revalidate: 300`):
- Pre-generates the first 10 posts at build time
- On demand, fetches the single post and its comments in parallel
- Returns `notFound: true` on fetch failure

Renders the post title, full body, comments section with count, and a back button. Handles the `isFallback` loading state.

### pages/api/posts/index.js (837 B)
API route for `GET` and `POST` at `/api/posts`:
- **GET**: Returns a simulated hardcoded array of 2 posts
- **POST**: Validates `title` and `content` in the body, returns 400 if missing, otherwise returns a simulated new post with `Date.now()` ID
- Other methods: Returns 405 with Allow header

### pages/api/posts/[id].js (984 B)
API route for `GET`, `PUT`, `DELETE` at `/api/posts/[id]`:
- **GET**: Returns a simulated post by ID
- **PUT**: Updates title/content, returns the updated post
- **DELETE**: Returns a success message
- Other methods: Returns 405 with Allow header

---

## 5. Files in `public/`

**There is no `public/` directory in this project.** The `_document.js` references `/favicon.ico`, but no `public/` directory exists to serve it.

---

## 6. Component/Layout Files Outside `pages/`

### components/AppProvider.js (495 B)
The only component file outside `pages/`. Creates a React Context (`AppContext`) with:
- `AppProvider` -- wraps children, provides `{ theme, setTheme }` state (defaults to `'light'`)
- `useAppContext` -- custom hook with guard that throws if used outside the provider

This provider is consumed by `_app.js` and wraps the entire application.

### styles/globals.css (1.4 KB)
Global stylesheet. Contains:
- CSS reset (box-sizing, margin, padding)
- Body font (Inter), colors, line-height
- App container (max-width 1200px, centered)
- Header styling with nav links in blue (`#0070f3`)
- Posts grid using CSS Grid (`auto-fit, minmax(300px, 1fr)`)
- Post cards with border and border-radius
- Comments styling
- Error page centered layout
- Button base styles (blue background, white text, hover darkens)

---

## 7. next.config.ts

File: `/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts` (104 B)

```ts
import type { NextConfig } from "next";

const nextConfig: NextConfig = {};

export default nextConfig;
```

It is a TypeScript config file using `NextConfig` type from Next.js 16. Currently empty -- no custom configuration at all. Note: This project uses `next.config.ts`, **not** `next.config.js` or `next.config.mjs`.

---

## 8. Middleware

**There is no `middleware.ts` file anywhere in the project.**

---

## 9. _app.tsx and _document.tsx

| File | Path | Size | Language |
|------|------|------|----------|
| `_app.js` | `/home/user/workspace/pages/_app.js` | 602 B | JavaScript |
| `_document.js` | `/home/user/workspace/pages/_document.js` | 584 B | JavaScript |
| `_error.js` | `/home/user/workspace/pages/_error.js` | 714 B | JavaScript |

All three special page files are plain `.js` (not `.tsx`). There are no TypeScript page files anywhere.

---

## 10. API Routes

Two API route files, both under `/pages/api/posts/`:

| Route | File | Methods | Size |
|-------|------|---------|------|
| `/api/posts` | `pages/api/posts/index.js` | GET, POST | 837 B |
| `/api/posts/[id]` | `pages/api/posts/[id].js` | GET, PUT, DELETE | 984 B |

Both use simulated/in-memory data (no database). They follow the standard Next.js Pages Router API route pattern with `export default function handler(req, res)`.

---

## 11. Patterns Used in Each Page File

### pages/index.js -- Home Page (SSR)
- **Data fetching**: `getServerSideProps` -- SSR on every request
- **External API**: Fetches from `https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts?_limit=5`
- **Request access**: Reads `req.headers['user-agent']`
- **Router**: Uses `useRouter` for imperative navigation (`router.push`)
- **Head**: Sets title, meta description, OG title
- **Rendering pattern**: Lists items with `posts.map()`, uses plain `<a>` links (not `<Link>`) for post links

### pages/blog/index.js -- Blog Listing (ISR)
- **Data fetching**: `getStaticProps` with `revalidate: 60` (Incremental Static Regeneration)
- **External API**: Fetches all posts from jsonplaceholder
- **Router**: Uses `useRouter` for imperative navigation on "Read More" buttons
- **Rendering pattern**: Grid of cards with `posts.map()`, uses `<a>` for title links and `<button>` + `router.push` for "Read More"

### pages/blog/[id].js -- Single Post (SSG + ISR + Fallback)
- **Data fetching**: `getStaticPaths` (pre-builds first 10 posts, `fallback: 'blocking'`) + `getStaticProps` with `revalidate: 300`
- **External API**: Fetches post + comments in parallel with `Promise.all`
- **Error handling**: Returns `notFound: true` on fetch failure
- **Fallback state**: Checks `router.isFallback` and renders a "Loading..." state
- **Router**: Uses `router.back()` for navigation
- **Head**: Sets dynamic title, description, OG tags

### pages/_app.js -- App Shell
- Imports global CSS
- Wraps `Component` with `AppProvider` context and a shared layout (header/main/footer)
- Uses `<Link>` from `next/link` for navigation
- Plain JS function component (no TypeScript)

### pages/_document.js -- HTML Shell
- Uses `next/document` imports: `Html, Head, Main, NextScript`
- Sets `<Html lang="en">`
- Loads Google Fonts (Inter: 400, 500, 600)
- Plain JS function component

### pages/_error.js -- Custom Error Page
- Uses `getInitialProps` (the older pattern, still used for error pages)
- Handles both server and client errors
- Renders status code and a contextual message

### pages/404.js -- Custom 404
- Client-side component with `useRouter`
- "Go Back Home" button using `router.push('/')`
- Sets head metadata for the 404 page

### API Routes
- Standard `export default function handler(req, res)` pattern
- Method-based routing via `req.method` checks
- Returns 405 with `Allow` header for unsupported methods
- Simulated data (no real persistence)

---

## Summary

This is a **Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41** project using the **Pages Router** (not App Router). It is a blog application with:

- **3 page routes**: Home (`/`, SSR), Blog index (`/blog`, ISR at 60s), Blog post (`/blog/[id]`, SSG + ISR at 300s + fallback blocking)
- **2 API routes**: `/api/posts` (GET/POST) and `/api/posts/[id]` (GET/PUT/DELETE)
- **3 special pages**: `_app.js`, `_document.js`, `_error.js`, plus a custom `404.js`
- **1 shared component**: `AppProvider` (React Context for theme state)
- **1 stylesheet**: `globals.css` (plain CSS, no CSS modules or Tailwind)
- **Config**: `next.config.ts` (empty), `tsconfig.json` (with `allowJs: true`)
- **No TypeScript in pages**: All page files are plain `.js`
- **No middleware, no public/ directory, no layouts/ directory**
- **External data source**: All data comes from `jsonplaceholder.typicode.com`
</output>

The migration plan:

1. **Read all existing files** to get the exact content
2. **Create App Router structure**:
   - `app/layout.tsx` (replaces `_app.js` and `_document.js`)
   - `app/page.tsx` (replaces `pages/index.js`)
   - `app/not-found.tsx` (replaces `pages/404.js` and `pages/_error.js` 404 case)
   - `app/error.tsx` (replaces `pages/_error.js` error case)
   - `app/blog/page.tsx` (replaces `pages/blog/index.js`)
   - `app/blog/[id]/page.tsx` (replaces `pages/blog/[id].js`)
   - `app/api/posts/route.ts` (replaces `pages/api/posts/index.js`)
   - `app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts` (replaces `pages/api/posts/[id].js`)
4. **Convert .js to .tsx/.ts** with proper types
5. **Convert Pages Router APIs**:
   - `getServerSideProps` → `fetch()` in a Server Component
   - `getStaticProps` → `fetch()` in a Server Component
   - `getStaticPaths` → `generateStaticParams`
   - `getInitialProps` → not needed in App Router (error handling via `error.tsx`)
   - `useRouter` for navigation → `useRouter` from `next/navigation` 
   - `Head` component → `metadata` export
   - API route `(req, res)` → `NextRequest`, `NextResponse`
6. **Convert AppProvider** to a proper Client Component if needed
7. **Remove pages directory**

text:
Now I have all the file contents. Let me plan the complete migration carefully.

## Migration Plan

### Pages Router → App Router Mapping

| Pages Router | App Router |
|---|---|
| `pages/_app.js` | `app/layout.tsx` — root layout with metadata, fonts, and AppProvider |
| `pages/_document.js` | `app/layout.tsx` — root layout handles HTML/body/fonts |
| `pages/_error.js` | `app/error.tsx` + `app/not-found.tsx` |
| `pages/404.js` | `app/not-found.tsx` |
| `pages/index.js` | `app/page.tsx` |
| `pages/blog/index.js` | `app/blog/page.tsx` |
| `pages/blog/[id].js` | `app/blog/[id]/page.tsx` |
| `pages/api/posts/index.js` | `app/api/posts/route.ts` |
| `pages/api/posts/[id].js` | `app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts` |

### Key App Router API replacements:

1. **`getServerSideProps`** → Direct `fetch()` in a Server Component
2. **`getStaticProps`** → Direct `fetch()` in a Server Component (cached by default)
3. **`getStaticPaths`** → `generateStaticParams`
4. **`getInitialProps`** → Not needed; use `error.tsx` and `not-found.tsx`
5. **`next/head`** `<Head>` → `metadata` export (for static metadata)
6. **`next/router` `useRouter`** → `next/navigation` `useRouter` (different API)
7. **`(req, res) => handler`** → `NextRequest` + `NextResponse`
8. **`_document.js`** → Root layout handles `<html>`, `<body>`, fonts
9. **`_app.js`** → Root layout with children wrapper + context providers

### File replacements (convert .js → .tsx/.ts):

**Source files to create:**

1. `components/AppProvider.tsx` — Convert to TypeScript + mark as Client Component
2. `app/layout.tsx` — Root layout (replaces `_app.js` + `_document.js`)
3. `app/page.tsx` — Home page (SSR via server component)
4. `app/not-found.tsx` — 404 page
5. `app/error.tsx` — Error page
6. `app/blog/page.tsx` — Blog listing
7. `app/blog/[id]/page.tsx` — Blog post detail
8. `app/api/posts/route.ts` — API route for /api/posts (GET, POST)
9. `app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts` — API route for /api/posts/[id] (GET, PUT, DELETE)

**Files to remove:**
Everything under `pages/`

Let me now think about each file in detail:

### 1. `components/AppProvider.tsx`
- Needs `'use client'` directive since it uses `useState`
- Convert to TypeScript with proper types
- Keep same functionality

### 2. `app/layout.tsx`
- Replaces both `_app.js` and `_document.js`
- Import global CSS
- Wrap children with `AppProvider`
- Render the header/nav/footer layout
- Set metadata via `metadata` export
- Use `Inter` font from `next/font` instead of Google Fonts link (better practice, eliminates FOUT)

Actually, for the font, using `next/font` is the recommended approach in App Router. Let me use that.

### 3. `app/page.tsx` (Home)
- Convert `getServerSideProps` to direct `fetch()` call
- For user-agent: use `headers()` from `next/headers`
- For timestamp: just compute it
- Use `next/link` instead of `router.push`
- Use `next/navigation` `useRouter` if needed (or just use Link)
- `metadata` export for head tags

### 4. `app/not-found.tsx`
- Replaces both `404.js` and the 404 case from `_error.js`
- Use `next/navigation` `useRouter` instead of `next/router` `useRouter`
- `metadata` export

### 5. `app/error.tsx`
- Replaces the general error case from `_error.js`
- App Router's `error.tsx` receives `error` and `reset` props
- `'use client'` directive required

### 6. `app/blog/page.tsx` (Blog listing)
- Convert `getStaticProps` to direct `fetch()`
- Use `Link` from `next/link` instead of `router.push` + button
- `metadata` export

### 7. `app/blog/[id]/page.tsx` (Blog post detail)
- Use `generateStaticParams` instead of `getStaticPaths`
- Convert `getStaticProps` to direct `fetch()`
- `metadata` export with dynamic title
- For the fallback loading state: use `loading.tsx` file
- For `router.isFallback`: not needed in App Router with `fallback: 'blocking'` equivalent behavior
- `router.back()` → still use `useRouter` but from `next/navigation`

### 8. `app/api/posts/route.ts`
- Use `NextRequest` / `NextResponse` pattern
- Export named functions: `export async function GET(req: NextRequest)`, `POST(req)`

### 9. `app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts`
- Same as above
- Extract `id` from `params` (which is a Promise in Next.js 16)

### Also need:
- `app/blog/[id]/loading.tsx` — loading state for fallback
- `app/loading.tsx` — general loading state (optional, might not need)

### Types to add:
- Post type
- Comment type
- Error page props

Let me think about the `_error.js` replacement carefully. The Pages Router `_error.js` serves as both 404 and 500 error page. In App Router:
- `not-found.tsx` handles 404s (including for pages that return `notFound()`)
- `error.tsx` handles errors (500, etc.)

So I need both.

For the `useRouter` in App Router for navigation:
- `router.push('/')` in Pages Router → `router.push('/')` in `next/navigation` (same call, different import)
- `router.back()` → `router.back()` in `next/navigation` (same call, different import)
- `router.isFallback` → Not available in App Router. Instead, `loading.tsx` handles loading states.

For the fallback pattern in `blog/[id]`:
- Pages Router: `getStaticPaths` with `fallback: 'blocking'` + `router.isFallback` check
- App Router: `generateStaticParams` + `loading.tsx`. With `fallback: 'blocking'`-equivalent behavior, if a path is not in `generateStaticParams`, Next.js will SSR it on demand. The loading state isn't needed for `blocking` mode since it blocks until ready.

For `getServerSideProps` that accesses `req.headers['user-agent']`:
- App Router: Use `headers()` from `next/headers` in the server component

Let me also think about the `metadata` export pattern. For pages with static metadata, export a `metadata` object. For dynamic metadata, export a `generateMetadata` function.

Now let me think about the ISR behavior:
- Pages Router `revalidate: 60` → In App Router, `fetch()` with `next: { revalidate: 60 }` 
- Pages Router `getStaticProps` with `revalidate` → In App Router ISR, just add `export const revalidate = 60`
- Or more precisely, `fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: 60 } })`

Actually in Next.js App Router, there are two ways to revalidate:
1. `fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: 60 } })` — segment-level
2. `export const revalidate = 60` — route segment config

For the blog listing page (`/blog`), I should use `fetch` with revalidation to match the ISR behavior.

For the blog detail page (`/blog/[id]`), same approach.

Let me also think about the `searchParams` in `getServerSideProps` — the home page doesn't use it, so fine.

For the API routes in App Router (Next.js 16), the request body parsing works differently:
- Pages Router: `req.body` is parsed by built-in body parser
- App Router: `req.json()` to parse JSON body

For dynamic route params in App Router (Next.js 16), the second argument to the handler has `params` as a Promise:
```typescript
export async function GET(request: NextRequest, { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }) {
  const { id } = await params;
  // ...
}
```

Wait, in Next.js 15+, params became asynchronous (a Promise). This was a breaking change. Let me use that pattern.

Let me finalize the file structure:

```
app/

    loading.tsx        ← Blog loading state

components/

styles/

```

Let me think about the metadata approach for each page:

**layout.tsx** (root layout):

```ts
export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: 'My Blog',
  description: 'A complex blog application',
  icons: { icon: '/favicon.ico' },
}
```

**app/page.tsx** (home):
```ts
export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: 'Home - My Blog',
  description: 'Welcome to my blog homepage',
  openGraph: { title: 'Home - My Blog' },
}
```

**app/blog/page.tsx** (blog listing):
```ts
export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: 'Blog - My Blog',
  description: 'All blog posts',
}
```

**app/blog/[id]/page.tsx** (dynamic):
```ts
export async function generateMetadata({ params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }): Promise<Metadata> {
  const { id } = await params;
  const post = await fetch(`https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/${id}`).then(r => r.json());
  return {
    title: `${post.title} - My Blog`,
    description: post.body.substring(0, 160),
    openGraph: {
      title: post.title,
      description: post.body.substring(0, 160),
    },
  }
}
```

**app/not-found.tsx**:
```ts
export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: '404 - Page Not Found',
}
```

Alright, let me also consider the error.tsx file. The original `_error.js` handled 404 and generic errors. In App Router:
- `not-found.tsx` handles 404 (triggered by `notFound()` call or unmatched route)
- `error.tsx` handles errors in server/client components

For `error.tsx`, I need to replicate the original's behavior of showing different messages based on the error. But since `error.tsx` doesn't get a `statusCode`, I'll show a generic message and the error details.

Actually, let me check: in App Router's `error.tsx`, the props are `{ error: Error & { digest?: string }, reset: () => void }`. The `error` object might have a `statusCode` property, but it's not guaranteed. Let me keep it generic.

For the 404 case, in the original:
- `_error.js` had a special case for 404
- `404.js` was a separate custom page

In App Router, `not-found.tsx` handles all 404s, including those triggered by `notFound()` in server components and unmatched routes. So I can put the 404 design in `not-found.tsx`.

For the error page, the original showed status code and a message. In App Router's `error.tsx`, I'll show the error message with a "Try again" button.

Now, should the AppProvider be converted to TypeScript? Yes, that's part of the migration since all source files should be .tsx/.ts.

Let me also think about layouts more carefully. In the original `_app.js`:
- `AppProvider` wraps everything
- A layout with header, main, and footer wraps the page content
- Global CSS is imported

In App Router's root layout (`app/layout.tsx`):
- The HTML and body tags are implicit (they're always there)
- I import global CSS at the top
- I wrap children with AppProvider
- I render the header/footer layout around `{children}`
- I set up fonts via `next/font`

For `next/font` with Inter:
```ts
import { Inter } from 'next/font/google'
const inter = Inter({ subsets: ['latin'] })
// Then <body className={inter.className}>
```

This replaces the Google Fonts preconnect and stylesheet links from `_document.js`.

text: Migration complete. Let me provide a thorough summary.

## Migration Complete ✅

All routes migrated from Pages Router to App Router, types added, pages/ directory removed. Build passes with zero errors.

### What changed

| Route | Pages Router (`*.js`) | App Router (`*.tsx`/`*.ts`) | Key changes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/` | `pages/index.js` + `getServerSideProps` | `app/page.tsx` | `fetch()` in server component; `headers()` from `next/headers` for user-agent |
| `/blog` | `pages/blog/index.js` + `getStaticProps` | `app/blog/page.tsx` | `fetch()` with `next: { revalidate: 60 }` for ISR |
| `/blog/[id]` | `pages/blog/[id].js` + `getStaticPaths` + `getStaticProps` | `app/blog/[id]/page.tsx` | `generateStaticParams` + `generateMetadata`; `loading.tsx` for fallback |
| `/api/posts` | `pages/api/posts/index.js` + `handler(req, res)` | `app/api/posts/route.ts` | `NextRequest`/`NextResponse` with named `GET`/`POST` exports |
| `/api/posts/[id]` | `pages/api/posts/[id].js` + `handler(req, res)` | `app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts` | Same pattern; params as `Promise` per Next.js 16 |
| Layout | `_app.js` + `_document.js` | `app/layout.tsx` | Combined; `next/font` replaces Google Fonts `<link>` |
| 404 | `404.js` + `_error.js` (404 branch) | `app/not-found.tsx` | App Router convention, `'use client'` |
| Error | `_error.js` (error branch) | `app/error.tsx` | App Router error boundary with `reset()` |
| `AppProvider` | `components/AppProvider.js` | `components/AppProvider.tsx` | `'use client'` + TypeScript interfaces |

### API substitutions

| Pages Router API | App Router replacement |
|---|---|
| `getServerSideProps` | Server Component `async` + direct `fetch()` |
| `getStaticProps` | Server Component `async` + `next: { revalidate }` |
| `getStaticPaths` | `generateStaticParams` |
| `getInitialProps` | `error.tsx` + `not-found.tsx` |
| `next/head` `<Head>` | `metadata` export / `generateMetadata` |
| `next/router` `useRouter` | `next/navigation` `useRouter` / `<Link>` |
| `handler(req, res)` (API) | `export async function GET(req: NextRequest, ...)` |
| `_document.js` Google Fonts | `next/font/google` `Inter` |
| `router.isFallback` | `loading.tsx` alongside the page |

### Routing summary (build output)

```
ƒ /                          dynamic (headers())
○ /blog                      1m ISR
● /blog/[id] (10 paths)      5m ISR
ƒ /api/posts                 API route
ƒ /api/posts/[id]            API route
```

**0 errors, 0 warnings, 15 static pages generated, all 12 source files now TypeScript.**
